ddt:Target.Name: RSAT4_ACU_eng_v15
ddt:Target.Description:
2015 Mai, 8th: C.BUTA Correction VEH for XJD (Host veh code $50) *Add the VEH for XHA (Host veh code $4C)
2015 Mai, 7th: C.BUTA *Add the VEH for XJD (Host veh code $4C) *Add the VEH for XJC (Host veh code $4D) *Add the VEH for XBB (Host veh code $4E) *Add the VEH for AS1 (Host veh code $4F)
2014 Mai, 26th: A.Raducanu *Add the VEH for XBA (Host veh code $4B) *Add the XBA and XHA project *Add the VEH for XGA (Host veh code $4A)
2013 July, 5th: A.Raducanu *Add DTC $908004: Internal failure *Removed the unused DTC from $9080 *Removed the unused DTC from $0500
2012 April, 23th: A.M. * Add H79II and modify VEH for X07 (pb hex to dec) * Delete DTCExtendedData.DTCOccurenceCounter in ReadDTCInformation.ReportExtendedData
2011 December, 5th : A.M. V10: * Add the DTC: driving side confirmation mismatch * Add the VEH for X07 (Host veh code) * Add the $21EF for Daimler * Add the curtains in the screens
2010 October,19th
From SW 2.40 to SW 3.10 the output of the switch status in service $21 $02 (ECU state synthesis) has been changed (i.e. inverted) to match the with TDRC1 requirements. Implementation in SW 3.10: 01b (1) means switch open (for buckle switches), 400 Ohm (for passenger presence sensor or inhibition switch). 10b (2) means switch closed (for buckle switches), 100 Ohm (for passenger presence sensor or inhibition switch). The change is only effective for the service $21 $02. The switch measurement and evaluation itself has not been changed. Other functionalities (detection of short circuit, etc.) are not affected.

Data.Name: CLEDEV For reset crash
Data.Comment: This key is used to allow the reset of the calculator

Data.Name: CLEDEV For crash_output
Data.Comment: This key is used to allow the sending of crash-output - Ask to passive safety UET (65620)

Data.Name: CT_SystemOperationTime
Data.Comment: Resolution of 50ms by Digit

Data.Name: CT_CrashCurve_Pretrig
Data.Comment: Data of Crash Curve Pretrigger 50ms before Crash detection
Data.Description:
Each value is coded on 2 bytes.

Period of acquisition is 0,5 ms

First sample (byte 1 and 2) is 30 ms before the crash.

Last data (byte 121 ans 122) is at T2 (Time of first event qualification)

Data.Name: CT_CrashCurve_Posttrig
Data.Comment: Data of Crash Curve Post Triger 100ms after Crash detection
Data.Description:
Each value is coded on 2 bytes.

Period of acquisition is 0,5 ms

First sample (byte 125 and 126) is collected at T2 + 0,5ms.

Last data (byte 405 ans 406) is at T2 + 70 ms

T2 is time of first crash event qualification

Data.Name: CT_TimerLOB
Data.Comment: Time between algorithm start and the detection of the loss of battery. 1ms by digit
Data.Description: 255 ($FF) is the no activation value

Data.Name: CT_TimerCrashoutputL1Activation
Data.Comment: Time between algorithm start and the activation of thecrash output level1. 1ms by digit
Data.Description: 255 ($FF) is the no activation value

Data.Name: CT_MileageOfVehicle
Data.Comment: Resolution of 0,01 km by digit

Data.Name: CT_BrakeInformation_T1
Data.Comment: T0 is time when the crash is detected

Data.Name: CT_FrontAlgo_Snapshot1
Data.Comment: Block description of the algorithm. Ask to DIESE department for definition of the content
Data.Description:
FRONT ALGORITHM DATA
Data recorded When is collected Byte number Details
Algorithm timer   BYTE0 MSByte - Time of threshold activation since start of front algorithm
- Resoution = 1ms/dig
BYTE1 LSByte
Fire Logics   BYTE2 MSByte - Activated fire logics
     - Bit0: Fire logic 1
     - …
     - Bit9: Fire logic 10
     - Bit10 - 15: unused
- meaning of fire logics is calibration specific
BYTE3 LSByte
X Velocity (1st sensor)   BYTE4 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4
      - in systems w/o ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
- calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE5 LSByte
X Velocity (2nd sensor)   BYTE6 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
      - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE7 LSByte
Y Velocity   BYTE8 MSByte - Velocity change in Y direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4
      - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0
- calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE9 LSByte
Left B-pillar velocity   BYTE10 MSByte - Velocity change of left 45° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise)
- calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE11 LSByte
Right B-pillar velocity   BYTE12 MSByte - Velocity change of right 45° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise)
- calculated from front algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE13 LSByte
Front Fire Flags   BYTE14   - Activated fire flags:
     - Bit 0: Frontal belt threshold
     - Bit 1: Frontal driver airbag threshold
     - Bit 2: Frontal passenger airbag threshold
     - Bit 3 - 7: unused
Front Plausibility   BYTE15   - Plausibility flags (calibration specific)
     - Bit 0: 1st plausibility rule
     - Bit 1: 2nd plausibility rule
     - Bit 2 - 7: not used
- maximum one bit is set (first fulfilled plausibility rule)
Sensor Status   BYTE16   - Status of sensors
     - Bit 0: X sensor defect (ASG4 or ADXL78)
     - Bit 1: Y sensor defect (ASG4)
     - Bit 2: X sensor defect (ADXL78)
     - Bit 3: 1 defect satellite (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 4: 2 defect satellites (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 5: Left B-pillar sensor defect (current sample)
     - Bit 6: Right B-pillar sensor defect (current sample)
     - Bit 7: unused
Vehicle speed   BYTE17   - Vehicle speed at start of frontal algorithm
- Resolution: 0.5km/h per digit
Index   BYTE18 Block index Index to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM
Checksum   BYTE19 Checksum Checksum for data valid check

Data.Name: CT_SideAlgo_TimerSnapshot1
Data.Comment: Collected at the activation of the first front algodecision flag. 1ms/dig
Data.Description: Value 255 : Timer is not started

Data.Name: CT_SideAlgo_Snapshot2
Data.Comment: Block description of the algorithm. Ask to DIESE department for definition of the content
Data.Description:
SIDE ALGORITHM DATA
Data recorded When is collected Byte number Details
X "Power"   BYTE0 MSByte - Integrated acceleration values in X direction (from ASG4 or ADXL78)
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE1 LSByte
left B-pillar gSat   BYTE2 MSByte - Low pass filtered velocity change of left B-pillar gSat
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE3 LSByte
right B-pillar gSat   BYTE4 MSByte - Low pass filtered velocity change of right B-pillar gSat
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE5 LSByte
left C-pillar gSat   BYTE6 MSByte - Low pass filtered velocity change of left C-pillar gSat
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE7 LSByte
right C-pillar gSatT   BYTE8 MSByte - Low pass filtered velocity change of right C-pillar gSat
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE9 LSByte
Y Velocity   BYTE10 MSByte - Low pass filtered velocity change of ECU Y channel (calculated from ASG4)
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE11 LSByte
Fire Flags   BYTE12   - Activated fire flags:
     - Bit 0: Left side (ECU-Y based)
     - Bit 1: Right side (ECU-Y based)
     - Bit 2: Left side (satellite based)
     - Bit 3: Right side (satellite based)
     - Bit 4 - 7: unused
Fire Logics   BYTE13   - Activated fire logics
     - Bit0: Fire logic 1
     - …
     - Bit5: Fire logic 6
     - Bit 6 - 7: unused
- meaning of fire logics is calibration specific
Algo Time pSat   BYTE14   - Timer based on pSat satellites
- Resolution: 1ms/dig
Algo Time gSat   BYTE15   - Timer based on gSat satellites
- Resolution: 1ms/dig
Side Plausibility   BYTE16   - Crash side and side plausibility
     - Bit 0: 0 = left side crash; 1 = right side crash
     - Bit 1: plausibility threshold left satellite row 1 (left gSat B-pillar)
     - Bit 2: plausibility threshold right satellite row 1 (right gSat B-pillar)
     - Bit 3: plausibility threshold left satellite row 2 (left pSat door or left gSat C-pillar)
     - Bit 4: plausibility threshold right satellite row 2 (right pSat door or right gSat C-pillar)
     - Bit 5: plausibility threshold Y channel defect (ASG4)
     - Bit 6: plausibility threshold X channel (ASG4 or ADXL78)
     - Bit 7: plausibility threshold (calibration specific)
Sensor Status   BYTE17   - Status of sensors
     - Bit 0: left satellite row 1 (left gSat B-pillar)
     - Bit 1: right satellite row 1 (right gSat B-pillar)
     - Bit 2: left satellite row 2 (left pSat door or left gSat C-pillar)
     - Bit 3: right satellite row 2 (right pSat door or right gSat C-pillar)
     - Bit 4: ECU Y channel defect (ASG4)
     - Bit 5: ECU X channel defect (ASG4 or ADXL78)
     - Bit 6- 7: unused
left pSat value   BYTE18   - value of left door pressure sensor
- Resolution: digits (sensor specific)
right pSat value   BYTE19   - value of right door pressure sensor
- Resolution: digits (sensor specific)
reservered   BYTE20   - filler bytes (not used)
reservered   BYTE21   - filler bytes (not used)
Index   BYTE22 Block index Index to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM
Checksum   BYTE23 Checksum Checksum for data valid check

REAR ALGORITHM DATA
Data recorded When is collected Byte number Details
Algorithm timer   BYTE0 MSByte - Time of threshold activation since start of rear algorithm
- Resoution = 1ms/dig
BYTE1 LSByte
X Velocity (1st sensor)   BYTE2 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ASG4
      - in systems w/o ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE3 LSByte
X Velocity (2nd sensor)   BYTE4 MSByte - Velocity change in X direction
      - in systems with ASG4: calculated from ADXL78
      - in systems w/o ASG4: always 0
- calculated on-going with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE5 LSByte
Left B-pillar velocity   BYTE6 MSByte - Velocity change of left 45° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise)
- calculated from rear algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE7 LSByte
Right B-pillar velocity   BYTE8 MSByte - Velocity change of right 45° B-pillar sensor (zero otherwise)
- calculated from rear algorithm start with forgetting factor
- Resoution: dig (sensor specific)
BYTE9 LSByte
Rear Fire Flags   BYTE10   - Activated fire flags:
     - Bit 0: 1st level rear threshold
     - Bit 1: 2nd level rear threshold
     - Bit 2 - 7: unused
Sensor Status   BYTE11   - Status of sensors
     - Bit 0: X sensor defect (ASG4 or ADXL78)
     - Bit 1: Y sensor defect (ASG4)
     - Bit 2: X sensor defect (ADXL78)
     - Bit 3: 1 defect satellite (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 4: 2 defect satellites (algo-internally qualified)
     - Bit 5- 7: unused
Rear Plausibility   BYTE12   - Plausibility flags (calibration specific)
     - Bit 0: 1st plausibility rule
     - Bit 1: 2nd plausibility rule
     - Bit 2 - 7: not used
- maximum one bit is set (first fulfilled plausibility rule)
Vehicle speed   BYTE13   - Vehicle speed at start of frontal algorithm
- Resolution: 0.5km/h per digit
Index   BYTE14 Block index Index to determine the order of the blocks written in NVM
Checksum   BYTE15 Checksum Checksum for data valid check
